comments: Approved. The contract scope is strictly bounded to generating a single evidence-based assessment report, tasks/v0.7-m6-b-team-environment-pilot-report.md, evaluating whether the V0.7 artifact taxonomy and protocol can support Team Mode coordination across heterogeneous AI coding environments. This aligns directly with the recon packet, which defines the objective as validating multi-agent, multi-developer collaboration under strict JSON handoff governance and explicitly frames the effort as a documentation-driven pilot with an expected output limited to that report. The contract's scope language stays at the assessment layer: it calls for demonstrating accommodation of multi-developer, multi-agent workflows with capability modeling and conflict prevention, not implementing those systems. The out_of_scope clause is correctly restrictive and matches the requested prohibitions: it explicitly forbids physical .agent-state migrations, implementation of functional multi-agent background orchestration code or CI hooks, expansion to formal rollouts, and modifications to active workflow/dispatch.json or iteration-state.json state models. The allowed_files, required_outputs, and stop_conditions reinforce those bounds by limiting primary implementation to the report artifact and preventing unauthorized file changes or orchestration build-out. No scope leakage, semantic contradiction, or missing exclusion was detected relative to the recon packet and requested contract boundaries.
